overview: Build a first-feedback version of an Agent-driven canvas image generation workflow. Users chat with an Agent in the right panel, the Agent creates a structured generation plan node on the tldraw canvas, the user confirms execution, and generated intermediate/reference/final images appear around the plan node. Ralph must execute one story per iteration, commit story changes, and keep secrets out of the repository.
goals: Add an Agent LLM configuration surface for one OpenAI-compatible chat or multimodal model.
Use DeepAgentsjs with a built-in canvas image planning skill and product-only whitelisted tools.
Let users create, revise, confirm, execute, cancel, and retry structured generation plans from an Agent conversation.
Represent Agent output as a custom tldraw plan node that tracks jobs, dependencies, progress, errors, and thumbnails.
Support batch and dependency-aware generation with generated anchor/reference images while preserving existing manual generation workflows.
Reuse existing generation records, assets, history, gallery, local storage, and provider behavior for actual image generation jobs.
nonGoals: Do not persist Agent chat messages in v1; keep them in the current browser session only.
Do not add arbitrary workflow graph editing, node-graph authoring, or a full table editor inside the plan node.
Do not group Gallery or generation history by Agent plan in v1.
Do not expose shell commands, real filesystem tools, or non-product tools to DeepAgentsjs.
Do not support multiple saved Agent LLM profiles, user accounts, cloud sync, billing, or collaborative Agent sessions.
Do not continue Agent runs after the WebSocket disconnects or the page refreshes.
successMetrics: A user can configure an Agent LLM once and see masked configuration after refresh.
A user can open the Agent tab, type a multi-image request, and receive a visible plan node on the canvas.
A user can select up to three canvas images and have them used as Agent message context or image-generation references according to the vision setting.
A user can confirm a plan and watch job statuses, thumbnails, intermediate anchor images, and final images update in both the chat and plan node.
A plan with generated character or style anchors runs in dependency order and places all visible images around the plan node.
Manual text generation, reference generation, history rerun, locate, download, gallery, provider configuration, and project autosave continue to work.
openQuestions: No product-level open questions remain for v1. Defaults: Agent chat is session-only, Agent LLM config is persisted, one plan can produce at most 16 total generated images including intermediate anchors, each job can use at most 3 references, dependency source jobs must generate exactly 1 output, and WebSocket disconnect cancels the active Agent run.

stack.frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + tldraw + Tailwind CSS + lucide-react
stack.database: SQLite using Drizzle ORM and better-sqlite3
stack.agent: DeepAgentsjs with a built-in canvas image planning skill and whitelisted product tools
stack.agentLlmProvider: One saved OpenAI-compatible chat or multimodal model configuration
stack.imageProvider: Existing OpenAI-compatible Images API plus Codex Responses image provider selection for image generation
stack.transport: WebSocket for Agent conversation, plan updates, execution progress, thumbnails, and cancellation
stack.storage: Host ./data or DATA_DIR for SQLite, assets, COS configuration, Codex tokens, provider secrets, and Agent LLM config

routes.name: Agent LLM Configuration
routes.purpose: Read and save one OpenAI-compatible Agent LLM configuration with masked secret readback.

routes.name: Agent WebSocket
routes.purpose: Run Agent conversation, plan creation/revision, confirmed execution, cancellation, retry, status, and thumbnail events.

routes.name: Text To Image
routes.purpose: Continue generating actual images for Agent jobs without changing existing manual semantics.

routes.name: Reference Image Generation
routes.purpose: Continue generating actual images from selected or generated reference images for Agent jobs.

routes.name: Project State
routes.purpose: Continue saving the tldraw snapshot, including persistent Agent plan nodes, without saving Agent chat messages.

uiNotes: Keep the existing manual generation experience intact by adding a right-panel tab switcher for Manual and Agent.
The Agent tab should feel like a working tool, not a marketing surface: compact conversation, selected-reference summary, default generation controls, and visible run state.
The Agent tab default controls include size, quality, and output format; natural language can override them per plan.
Use the current selected canvas images as message attachments/reference candidates, capped at 3.
When Agent LLM supports vision, selected images can be sent to the Agent as multimodal input; when it does not, selected images are only available as image-generation references.
The custom plan node should be readable at a glance and show plan title, total output count, jobs, dependencies, progress, errors, thumbnails, and actions.
Final and intermediate generated images remain ordinary tldraw image shapes so users can move, crop, download, and reuse them as references.
Generated images should be placed around the plan node instead of centered in the viewport.
Desktop and mobile layouts must keep text and controls inside their containers with no overlapping UI.
rules: Ralph must complete exactly one story per iteration and commit story changes unless explicitly run with no-commit.
Use pnpm install if dependencies are missing; the package manager is pinned to pnpm@9.14.2.
Run pnpm typecheck and pnpm build before completing each story.
Frontend stories must perform browser verification against the running app, usually with pnpm dev and http://localhost:5173.
Keep local agent scratch files under .codex-temp/ and do not commit .ralph, .env, data, generated images, SQLite databases, build output, or machine-specific paths.
Secrets must only be read from .env, runtime environment, existing local provider config, Codex token storage, or the new Agent LLM config; secrets must never be logged.
Agent chat messages are session-only in v1 and must not be saved to the database, project API, or plan node.
Agent LLM configuration is persisted locally and returned to the browser only with masked API key data.
DeepAgentsjs must only receive whitelisted product tools for plan creation, plan update, job execution, selected reference access, canvas status updates, and cancellation.
Do not expose shell, arbitrary filesystem, environment inspection, or database inspection tools to the user-facing Agent.
A single GenerationPlan can request at most 16 total generated images, including intermediate character, style, or reference anchor images.
Each GenerationJob can use at most 3 resolved reference images, matching the existing edit/reference API limit.
Any job whose output is referenced by a downstream job must have count exactly 1.
Confirmed plans are structurally immutable during execution; if the Agent needs a different prompt, count, dependency, or reference, it must create a revised plan for user confirmation.
Unexecuted plan revisions update the same plan node; revisions after execution create a new plan node.
WebSocket disconnect or browser refresh cancels the active Agent run; already generated assets and canvas images remain.
Retry failed retries failed jobs plus downstream jobs blocked by those failures; do not rerun already successful independent jobs.
Generated intermediate images are visible on the canvas, labeled or represented as intermediate/reference assets in the plan node, and count against the 16-image limit.
Existing manual text generation, reference generation, generation history, Gallery, provider configuration, COS backup, and project autosave behavior must not regress.

stories.title: Add Agent LLM configuration and WebSocket foundation
stories.description: As a creator, I want to configure one OpenAI-compatible Agent LLM and establish a safe WebSocket channel so the Agent can later chat, plan, execute, and cancel without exposing secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add shared contracts for Agent LLM config view, save request, masked secret, WebSocket client messages, and WebSocket server events.
Add SQLite-backed Agent LLM configuration storage with one active row containing apiKey, baseUrl, model, timeoutMs, supportsVision, createdAt, and updatedAt.
Initialize or migrate the Agent LLM config table at startup using the existing database.ts migration style.
Implement GET /api/agent-config returning the saved config with API key masked and supportsVision visible.
Implement PUT /api/agent-config with API key replacement/preservation semantics, base URL/model trimming, positive timeout validation, and boolean supportsVision persistence.
Reject invalid config payloads with 400 JSON errors and do not log API key values.

Add an Agent WebSocket endpoint at /api/agent/ws that accepts connections, sends a connected event, handles ping/pong, handles cancel_run idempotently, and returns a clear missing-config error for Agent work when no usable Agent LLM config exists.
WebSocket close or disconnect must cancel any active in-memory run tied to that socket.
Negative case: missing Agent LLM config does not crash the server and produces a clear user-facing error event.
Negative case: malformed WebSocket JSON produces an error event without terminating the server process.
Verification: deterministic backend smoke checks or focused tests cover config save/readback, masking, preservation, invalid payloads, and basic WebSocket connect/error/cancel behavior.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Define GenerationPlan contracts and DeepAgentsjs planning skill
stories.description: As a creator, I want the Agent to turn a natural-language image request into a strict, inspectable GenerationPlan so the canvas can display and execute the plan deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add DeepAgentsjs as the Agent framework dependency and wire it through a backend planner module.
Define shared GenerationPlan, GenerationJob, GenerationReference, plan status, job status, dependency edge, plan defaults, and validation result contracts.
Implement a built-in versioned canvas image planning skill/system instruction that constrains the Agent to output strict GenerationPlan JSON.

The planning skill must explain the 16 total generated image cap, the 3 reference image cap per job, dependency source count=1, visible intermediate anchor images, and confirmation-before-execution rule.
The planner accepts user text, current Agent defaults, and up to 3 selected canvas reference summaries.

When supportsVision is true, selected images may be sent to the Agent as multimodal input; when false, selected images are represented as reference handles only and the Agent must not claim to visually inspect them.
Validate every plan server-side before sending it to the client.

Reject plans that exceed 16 total generated images including anchors, use more than 3 references per job, reference unknown jobs, create dependency cycles, or give a downstream-referenced source job count other than 1.
If the user asks for a character/story and no user image is supplied, the Agent may create one visible character_anchor job with count 1 and downstream references.
Negative case: invalid or non-JSON model output is converted into a clear error event and no plan node is created.

Verification: focused tests or deterministic smoke scripts cover valid simple plan, multi-prompt plan, selected-reference plan, generated-anchor dependency plan, over-limit plan rejection, cycle rejection, and no-vision reference handling.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Build Agent tab with selected reference inputs
stories.description: As a creator, I want a right-panel Agent tab where I can chat, set default output parameters, and attach selected canvas images so advanced generation starts from the same workspace context.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a compact right-panel tab switcher for Manual and Agent without removing or regressing the existing manual generation UI.
Agent tab includes a session-only conversation transcript, message input, send button, run/cancel affordance, and clear empty/config-missing states in Chinese.
Agent tab includes default size, quality, and output format controls using existing size/quality/format options.
Agent tab shows current selected canvas image references capped at 3, with file/name or fallback label and dimensions when available.
If more than 3 canvas images are selected, show a Chinese warning that only up to 3 can be used and do not send 4 or more as references.

If the Agent LLM config has supportsVision enabled, message submission includes selected image data or data URLs only for the capped references; otherwise it includes reference handles/summaries only.
Sending a user message opens the WebSocket if needed and renders assistant_delta, plan_created, error, run_cancelled, and run_done events in the Agent tab.
Agent-generated image thumbnails shown in chat can be clicked to locate the corresponding canvas image once AG-005 provides output shape IDs.
Manual text generation, manual reference generation, history actions, provider settings, home routing, gallery, and mobile drawer continue to behave as before.
Mobile and desktop layouts keep the Agent transcript, controls, and input inside the panel or drawer without overlapping the canvas or CTA.

Browser verification: verify desktop and mobile layouts, missing Agent config, text-only model mode, supportsVision mode with selected references, 0/1/3/4 selected reference states, and no regression to Manual mode.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Add canvas Agent plan node shape
stories.description: As a creator, I want Agent output to become a visible custom canvas node so I can inspect the plan, understand dependencies, and confirm execution in the same spatial workspace as the results.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a tldraw custom shape util for an Agent plan node and register it through the existing shapeUtils path.
The plan node stores the current GenerationPlan, stable dimensions, selectedJobId if needed, and lastRunId if needed.
The plan node renders title, total generated image count, plan status, job rows, role labels, dependency/reference chips, per-job status, concise error copy, and thumbnail slots.
The plan node provides Execute Plan, Cancel, and Retry Failed actions as embedded controls or selected-node controls with accessible labels.
When a plan_created event arrives, create a plan node in the visible canvas near the current viewport center or selected context.
When a plan_updated event arrives for an unexecuted plan, update the same plan node.
When a plan_updated event represents a revision of an already executed plan, create a new plan node instead of mutating the executed plan node.
Plan nodes persist through the existing project snapshot save/restore flow.
Autosave must not preserve a misleading in-progress run state after refresh; restored active/loading plan nodes should be normalized to a cancelled or idle/error state with no eternal spinner.
Negative case: a malformed plan node payload in an old or corrupted snapshot must not crash app load.

Browser verification: create a plan from Agent chat, refresh to confirm node restoration, revise before execution, revise after execution to create a new node, and verify node controls are visible on desktop and mobile.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Execute Agent plans with dependency-aware canvas output
stories.description: As a creator, I want confirmed Agent plans to run with dependency-aware execution so intermediate anchors and final outputs appear around the plan node with live status and retry support.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement product-only DeepAgentsjs execution tools for reading confirmed plan state, executing a generation job, updating plan/job status, creating canvas placeholders, attaching output assets, and cancelling the run.
Execution must not allow the Agent to change confirmed plan structure; prompt, count, dependencies, references, size, quality, and output format are fixed after confirmation.
Implement DAG scheduling where jobs with no unmet dependencies may run concurrently and jobs with dependencies wait for successful upstream outputs.
If an upstream job fails, downstream dependent jobs are marked blocked and are not executed until retry.
Resolve generated_output references by taking the single output of upstream dependency source jobs; dependency source jobs must already be validated as count=1.
Resolve selected_canvas_image references from the capped selected references captured when the plan was created or revised.
Each actual generation job uses the existing image generation/edit provider flows, local asset saving, COS backup if configured, and GenerationRecord persistence.
Intermediate anchor images and final images are both inserted as ordinary tldraw image shapes arranged around the plan node in job order.
The plan node receives live job_started, job_completed, job_failed, job_blocked, asset_preview, run_cancelled, and run_done updates.
The Agent chat receives compact status messages and generated thumbnails; clicking a thumbnail locates the corresponding output image shape.
Cancel stops outstanding requests, marks remaining running/queued jobs cancelled or blocked, and prevents cancelled results from being inserted after cancellation.
Retry Failed retries failed jobs plus downstream blocked jobs while preserving successful independent outputs and not rerunning completed jobs.
Negative case: if all jobs fail or are blocked, the plan node and Agent chat show a clear Chinese failure summary without leaving loading placeholders forever.

Browser verification: run a simple no-reference plan, a multi-prompt plan, a selected-reference plan, a generated character-anchor dependency plan, cancel in-flight execution, and retry a failed or blocked plan when possible.
Verification: pnpm typecheck and pnpm build pass.

stories.title: Document Agent workflow and complete verification handoff
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want the Agent workflow documented and fully verified so the six-story Ralph task leaves a usable first-feedback product surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Update README.md and README.zh-CN.md with Agent LLM setup, Agent tab usage, selected reference behavior, supportsVision behavior, plan node lifecycle, execution confirmation, cancel, retry, and known v1 limits.
Document that Agent chat messages are session-only in v1 and are not persisted after refresh.
Document that Agent LLM config is saved locally in DATA_DIR SQLite and should be treated as sensitive local runtime data.
Document the 16 total generated image cap, 3 references per job cap, dependency source count=1 rule, and WebSocket disconnect cancellation behavior.
Document that Gallery/history remain per-generation-record and are not grouped by Agent plan in v1.
Run pnpm typecheck successfully.
Run pnpm build successfully.

Run pnpm dev and browser-verify the existing manual text generation path, manual reference generation path, Agent config flow, Agent tab, selected reference caps, plan creation, plan node persistence, execution, cancellation, retry, thumbnails, and thumbnail-to-canvas locating.
Verify desktop and mobile layouts for the Agent tab and plan node controls.
Check browser console, API terminal, and Vite terminal for newly introduced runtime errors without printing secrets.
If live provider credentials or Agent LLM credentials are unavailable, record the exact environmental blocker in Ralph run notes without reading, printing, or committing secrets.
Confirm git status excludes .env, .ralph, .codex-temp, data, generated images, SQLite databases, screenshots, and build output from commits.
Verification: final story records commands run, browser findings, changed files, and residual risks.
verificationNotes: Updated README.md and README.zh-CN.md with Agent LLM setup, Agent tab usage, selected reference behavior, supportsVision behavior, plan node lifecycle, execution confirmation, cancel, retry, v1 caps, privacy notes, and troubleshooting.
Ran pnpm typecheck successfully after the final docs and mobile drawer accessibility fix.

Ran pnpm --filter @gpt-image-canvas/api smoke:planner, pnpm --filter @gpt-image-canvas/api smoke:agent, and pnpm --filter @gpt-image-canvas/api smoke:executor successfully. The smoke:agent and smoke:executor runs emitted only the Node punycode deprecation warning.
Ran pnpm build successfully. Vite emitted the existing Node 22.11.0 version warning and large chunk warning.

Browser-verified http://127.0.0.1:5173/canvas on desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844 using the existing dev server on port 5173. Manual tab and Agent tab rendered without overlap; Agent config state, selected reference cap display, and mobile drawer behavior were visible.
Browser console had no JavaScript runtime errors after the final fix. Remaining non-blocking console output: tldraw zh-cn missing message warnings and a form field id/name accessibility issue.

Live Agent planning with the current local fake vision config and selected large app preview previously returned HTTP 400 in this environment; no secrets were read or printed. Planner and executor behavior were verified with deterministic smoke scripts and fake image provider coverage.
Changed files for AG-006: README.md, README.zh-CN.md, apps/web/src/App.tsx, and .agents/tasks/prd-agent-canvas-generation.json.
Pre-commit git status contained only the AG-006 source/docs/PRD files; .env, .ralph, .codex-temp, data, generated images, SQLite databases, screenshots, and build output were not staged.
